title: Move blockade marker authority across an OS identity boundary
goal: Turn the typed blockade lifecycle from a same-UID policy boundary into a kernel-enforced authority boundary that arbitrary operator-UID code, existing tmux panes and out-of-band local processes cannot bypass.

acceptance.assertion: The canonical marker and its parent are owned by root or a dedicated service identity and are not writable, replaceable or unlinkable by the Grabowski operator UID.
Engage, exact hash-bound disarm, quarantine and restore are exposed only through a narrow broker contract with explicit action allowlists, caller identity, audit binding and fail-closed readback.

Live tests prove that arbitrary terminal, durable-job, persistent-task, operation, fleet-local and existing tmux execution under the operator UID cannot create, replace, remove or restore the canonical marker, including paths assembled dynamically at runtime.
Cutover from the user-owned marker path is atomic, reversible and hash-bound; crashes leave either the old verified state or the new verified broker-owned state, never two conflicting authorities.
The environment kill switch remains outside in-band broker disarm authority and continues to require external administrative clearing.
Focused security tests, full validation, diff-bound review, deployment provenance and live denial/readback receipts pass without using the production marker for destructive test setup.

metadata.source: Diff-bound self-review of heimgewebe/grabowski PR #235 on 2026-07-16
metadata.finding: Same-UID arbitrary code can construct the user-owned marker path without presenting it literally in argv, and pre-existing tmux or out-of-band processes are outside the typed tool policy boundary.
metadata.decision: Do not overclaim T001. Merge its scoped policy and recovery improvements only with an explicit boundary statement; retain initiative completion until this OS-separated authority task is complete.
does_not_establish: permission to weaken typed recovery evidence
permission to expose a broad root shell
permission to clear the environment kill switch in band
metadata.ready_reason: T001 is merged, deployed and live-verified; the remaining scope is the separate OS-identity authority boundary.
